items.title: Historic flour mill walls demolished despite grassroots fight to save Mount Gambier structure
items.summary: In the face of protests, the remnants of a historic flour mill in Mount Gambier are now a pile of crumbled stone. The site will be developed into a multi-storey hotel complex and townhouses.

items.title: Joe Ingles signs with NBL side Melbourne United for 2026-27 season
items.summary: The legendary Boomers and NBA star will return to Australia's NBL for the first time since 2009, signing with Melbourne United for two years.

items.title: UK's Channel 4 removes all Married at First Sight episodes after BBC reports sexual assault allegations
items.summary: British broadcaster Channel 4 has removed all previous seasons of Married at First Sight after rape allegations came to light.

items.title: Bernard Tomic edges closer to shock French Open return as fellow Australians struggle in qualifiers
items.summary: Bernard Tomic is just two wins from reaching the main draw of the French Open since 2019 after being the only Australian to enjoy success in the qualifiers.

items.title: Community in Victoria's south-east fights back against Sandy Point caravan park development proposal
items.summary: A community group in Victoria's south-east will appeal a council planning decision to approve the development of a regional caravan park, which it says would be an "absolute disaster" for the region.

items.title: Live: ASX set to jump on Iran peace hopes
items.summary: The futures market tips the ASX 200 to jump more than 1 per cent as a shaky peace holds in the Middle East. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.summary: Fire crews were called to a blaze in Glenroy that gutted several shops. Locals say there have been multiple suspicious fires in the area.

items.title: Prime minister knocks back $94 million proposal to help flooded farmers restock
items.summary: Widespread flooding in north-west Queensland killed more than 90,000 head of livestock this year, but farmers will have to go it alone if they want to rebuild.

items.summary: Almost 50 high school students and teachers have been rescued after becoming stranded by floodwater at a national park in the Scenic Rim.

items.title: Batchelor locals call for relocation of evacuation centre as NT government weighs up options

items.title: Motor neurone disease research to begin in Toowoomba after community fundraising effort

items.title: Millicent National Trust SA branch locked in $230,000 property dispute as opposition flags inquiry

items.summary: Pouria Zaratifoukolaei, who works for a Persian-language outlet critical of the Iranian government, was attacked near his home in London in 2024.

items.title: First home buyers buoyed by negative gearing, capital gains changes at first Saturday auctions since budget
items.summary: The government says the changes are aimed at levelling the playing field for young buyers bidding against property investors, while the opposition is pledging to undo the reforms, citing their potential to raise rents.

items.title: NSW government funds Aboriginal-led south coast sea urchin enterprise with $1.48m grant
items.summary: After decades of fighting for their traditional fishing rights, Aboriginal custodians are now fighting to save their sea country from ecological collapse.

items.title: Sam Kerr ties Chelsea's goals record in final Women's Super League match
items.summary: Sam Kerr ties the record for the most Women's Super League goals for Chelsea as she plays her final match with the London club after more than six years.

items.title: Rewiring Australia campaign for renters to be included in electrical future
items.summary: A standard power plug for charging your home from an EV is among the measures proposed to improve energy standards for rental properties.

items.title: Malnourished and overtrained, Olympic gymnast didn't have energy for a period
items.summary: Alexandra Kiroi-Bogatyreva trained up to 10 hours a day, six days a week, often without the proper nutrition. She wants others to know what that can do to the body.

items.title: Aspiration has changed since the Howard era. This budget is finally catching up
items.summary: At the turn of the century, "aspiration" described Australians who wanted to put their kids into private school and university, and to build their wealth by getting an investment property or two. Today, it means something humbler; millions of younger Australians simply aspire to own a home one day, to have some shelter.

items.title: Donald Trump discusses a renovation project at Washington DC's reflecting pool
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: 'Accidental leader' of CFA levy protest Rob Armstrong reveals scars left by childhood bushfire trauma
items.summary: Every time farmer and volunteer firefighter Rob Armstrong gets a call to battle a bushfire, something inside says not to do it. But he goes anyway. He knows what it's like to be trapped in the flames and rescued by volunteers.

items.title: What do governments need to do this time to avoid pre-NDIS mistakes?
items.summary: The states are once again on the hook for disability services due to the Commonwealth's drastic NDIS cuts. Can the mistakes of the past be avoided?

items.title: The spiral-shaped trap: AI chatbots and the descent into delusion
items.summary: Rodrigues thought an AI chatbot could help him escape poverty and build wealth. Instead, he became trapped in a spiral of delusion and dependence.

items.title: Delta Goodrem takes fourth spot in Eurovision 2026 as Bulgaria wins
items.summary: Delta Goodrem’s vocals secure a fourth-place finish for Australia in the annual contest, with Bulgaria taking top spot.

items.title: Rochelle Jordan on Through The Wall, critical acclaim and learning to love her own music
items.summary: The Canadian singer won widespread acclaim with her third album. Now she brings it to Australia.

items.title: Khaled Sabsabi's new installations 'didn't have a home'. Now they're at the Venice Biennale
items.summary: Last year, Khaled Sabsabi was appointed, sacked and then reinstated as Australia's representative to the Venice Biennale. Now he opens two installations at the world's most prestigious art exhibition.

items.title: Can you score top marks on Guy Montgomery's spelling quiz?
items.summary: Are you ready to test your spelling skills like your little lunch depends on it? Come on in.

items.title: Sydney FC defeats Newcastle Jets on penalties to progress to A-League Men grand final
items.summary: Eli Adams's 96th-minute equaliser proves to be in vain as Sydney FC downs Newcastle on penalties to progress to the A-League Men season decider.

items.title: Labor's Luke Richmond on track to win by-election in Brisbane seat of Stafford
items.summary: At the end of counting on Saturday night, there had been a 4.1 per cent swing against Labor after preferences, with additional postal votes still to be counted.

items.title: Melbourne City wins A-League Women's championship as Matildas star Holly McNamara shines against Wellington Phoenix
items.summary: Sensational strikes from young stars Holly McNamara and Leticia McKenna have helped Melbourne City to a record-equalling fifth A-League Women championship.

items.title: Virat Kohli is ready to play for India at 2027 World Cup, but only if he adds something to the team
items.summary: Virat Kohli says if he plays in the 2027 ICC World Cup he will compete for "every ball like it's the last ball" - but vows if he can't add value to India's team he will not be there.

items.title: Angus Taylor vows to amend Sex Discrimination Act following 'Tickle v Giggle' case
items.summary: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor has vowed to amend the Sex Discrimination Act after the Federal Court upheld a landmark ruling that the exclusion of a transgender woman from a female-only app was discriminatory.

items.title: Boy dead, two seriously injured following car crash in north-west Victoria
items.summary: Police say a car stolen from Mildura last night was travelling along the Calder Highway when it veered off the road and struck a tree.

items.title: Origin hopefuls stand up as Cowboys coach hails 'gritty' win
items.summary: Todd Payten said his side's victory over the Roosters was "as good a win as I've had" with the club as Melbourne notched back-to-back wins after a messy 34-8 victory over Parramatta and the Sea Eagles thrash Wests Tigers in Magic Round at Lang Park.

items.title: How Narendra Modi's government is shaping India into a one-party state
items.summary: How Prime Minister Narendra Modi is turning India into a one-party nation.

items.title: Scottish FA condemns 'vigilantism' after referee's details leak online for controversial penalty
items.summary: Scottish FA condemns "vigilantism" after a referee's personal details are leaked online following a highly controversial penalty in the Scottish Premier League.

items.title: Blues claim spirited victory over Dogs to begin post-Voss life in style
items.summary: Carlton beats the Western Bulldogs by 12 points in its first game since Michael Voss left the club, while Melbourne upsets Hawthorn in style. Follow live.

items.title: Where to watch Eurovision in Australia, how to vote and what to know about Delta Goodrem's song
items.summary: The Eurovision Song Contest is on this Sunday, which will mean an early start for superfans of the pop culture phenomenon.

items.title: A hearty slow-cooked beef stew that will warm you up as the weather cools - ABC News
items.summary: Just 15 minutes of prep means your slow cooker will have a rich beef stew waiting at dinner time.

items.title: Farmers in limbo as $100m in Inland Rail land sits idle
items.summary: For more than a decade, Australians in the path of the proposed Inland Rail corridor have wondered if the nation-building project will force them from their homes. Although the northern section has been scrapped, the legacy of its blueprints is still fuelling anxiety.

items.title: Bill to support environmental water flows an 'attack' on property rights
items.summary: Politicians and landholders vow to fight a controversial new New South Wales law that will allow the inundation of private property from environmental water flows under the Murray-Darling Basin Plan.

items.title: Residents fear Oyster Bay shallowing out and becoming mudflat
items.summary: A bin that has been sticking out of the mud for 12 years has become a symbol for an ongoing issue of sediment build-up in Oyster Bay in Sydney's Sutherland Shire.

items.title: Passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship arrive in Perth
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Hantavirus-hit cruise ship Australians isolating in Perth unlikely to be paid out, experts say
items.summary: But the lawyer who led the class action in the COVID-19 era Ruby Princess case has warned the cruise company will likely argue Australian law had no jurisdiction over the matter.

items.title: First Nations father teaches dying Indigenous language to sons through bedtime story
items.summary: Five years ago, Gudjala man Braithen Knox began learning his mob's language, which has fewer than 10 speakers. Now a father of two, he is passing it on to his sons through bedtime stories.

items.title: Couple's warning after losing everything, including two dogs, in devastating fire
items.summary: Patrick and Susan Doyle recently lost almost everything in a house fire, including their beloved dogs, Oscar and Mercy. While the couple escaped with their lives, Mrs Doyle was badly burnt. They are warning others to be better prepared.

items.title: Albanese government's response to gambling advertising inquiry criticised as 'timid'
items.summary: Under the cover of budget day, the Albanese government this week dropped its long-awaited response to a gambling harm inquiry. Advocates say it has "failed miserably".

items.title: Tiger shark beheading in Albany prompts call for greater regulation
items.summary: A researcher speaks out against the fishing of sharks after footage of a huge tiger shark being caught and decapitated circulated on social media.

items.title: This Place: Terry Hie Hie
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Aunty Polly Cutmore shares her family's stories of survival at Dhirri-aay-aay
items.summary: Back on country, Aunty Polly Cutmore says she feels like a child again when visiting her ancestral lands. Now, as a respected Goombri elder, she shares the stories of her country.

items.title: Widow's campaign for stronger gambling regulations after her husband's death
items.summary: Annie Ashton's husband took his own life after suffering from a gambling addiction. Since then, she's campaigned for stronger regulation of the gambling industry and greater awareness of gambling harm.

items.title: Mature Artists Dance Experience for over 50s hopes for increased arts funding ahead of Tasmanian budget
items.summary: As the social and health benefits of a not-for-profit dance group for over 50s are felt, industry insiders hope more funding will be invested in the arts sector ahead of the Tasmanian state budget.

items.title: US President Donald Trump, Chinese President Xi Jinping end unipolar age in Beijing
items.summary: This week in Beijing, Donald Trump and Xi Jinping quietly admitted something the world has been slow to grasp: neither can afford a collision between their two countries.

items.title: A tiny nesting box in Ireland is my antidote to the despair of adulthood
items.summary: This little box of joy is a microcosm, a mini-exemplar of what a small amount of local effort can do to help fix some of our damage of the natural world.

items.title: Cruel dismissal denies Mitch Marsh IPL century as Lucknow Super Giants defeat Chennai Super Kings
items.summary: Mitch Marsh receives a nightmare slice of luck after a stunning innings put him on course for the triple figures.

items.title: SANFL kicking goals with growing viewership as AFL draws criticism
items.summary: SANFL crowds are continuing to climb each year after COVID-19 restrictions put a dent in numbers, but its broadcast reach is winning ratings, and streaming has reached new heights.

items.title: US President Donald Trump leaves Beijing with no major breakthroughs on Iran war
items.summary: US President Donald Trump left China on Friday without securing tangible help from Beijing to end the Iran war, but praised China's President, Xi Jinping, during his visit.

items.title: Counting underway in by-election in Brisbane seat of Stafford, swing towards LNP
items.summary: Labor candidate Luke Richmond wants to make the contest a referendum on health, while LNP candidate Fiona Hammond is focused on fuel, the cost of living, and crime.

items.title: How Wallabies winger Dylan Pietsch uses art to connect with his culture
items.summary: While Dylan Pietsch is a star on the rugby field for the Wallabies and Western Force, he turns to his love of painting to express himself and be creative.

items.title: Diphtheria used to be a major cause of childhood deaths. Now it's back in Australia
items.summary: Once a major cause of childhood deaths, diphtheria was largely eliminated through vaccination. Health authorities say falling immunity may now be helping the disease spread again.

items.title: Migrant nurses training to fill skills gaps in rural SA health project
items.summary: The rural town of Cleve in SA is getting a much-needed boost to its health system with a pilot program sponsoring five nurses from the Philippines to undertake traineeships to become enrolled nurses.

items.title: Road safety expert says country 'decades behind' after second fatal Gumlu bus crash
items.summary: A retired traffic engineer says Australia is "decades behind" in road safety standards and needs an overhaul after a second fatal bus crash on the same stretch of the Bruce Highway.

items.title: Young Australian Rules players eye US college football instead of AFL draft
items.summary: A new generation of Australian athletes is skipping the MCG entirely, eyeing US college football scholarships instead of Aussie Rules.

items.title: How WA's once-revered donkeys became 'nuisance' pests and canned meat
items.summary: Before roads and vehicles, teams of donkeys used to haul huge loads across remote parts of Western Australia. But the beloved animal became a declared pest that even the meat trade couldn't save.

items.title: Natural burials begin at NSW conservation cemetery Walawaani Way
items.summary: After nearly 10 years getting public health and council approvals, a natural burial ground on the NSW south coast begins letting people have a simpler, more environmentally friendly farewell.

items.title: What the undersea monitoring device found in Indonesia tells us about China's maritime strategy
items.summary: The discovery of a Chinese undersea monitoring device in a strategic waterway "shows the extent of both China's reach and its undersea ambitions" and has implications for Australia's defence strategy, say experts.

items.title: Bronze bust of Scott Morrison joins Prime Ministers Avenue in Ballarat
items.summary: A thinner ex-PM cracks jokes as his bust is revealed, making him the 30th Australian leader with a likeness in Ballarat's Prime Ministers Avenue.

items.title: The Interview: Jane Caro reckons Australia is kidding itself about private schools
items.summary: "There are absolutely no statistics that say that because you sent your kid to a private school they're going to do better in life."

items.title: Wayne Swan slams the 'view from Mar-a-Lago' as battle for middle Australia gets real
items.summary: Labor and the Coalition are both acutely mindful of One Nation's rise and its determination to take a decisive role in a future conservative government.

items.title: Sam Coulton turned his family's cotton crop into a clothing brand now stocked across Australia
items.summary: Queensland cotton farmer and entrepreneur Sam Coulton has spent decades diversifying beyond agriculture, building a fashion brand and tourism business in Goondiwindi while creating jobs and investing in the future of his rural town.

items.title: Netanyahu's rare interview put Gulf ties and Israel's domestic politics on display
items.summary: The question for the Gulf states is whether they are happy for Israel to be the dominant player in the Middle East.

items.title: Most widespread May rain in a decade to reach all Australian states
items.summary: The most widespread May rain in a decade is about to sweep across Australia as the first north-west cloudband of 2026 becomes a reality.

items.title: Neo-Nazis fundraising to challenge laws that listed them as hate group
items.summary: The leader of Neo-Nazi group National Socialist Network, Thomas Sewell, has appeared in an interview online alongside notorious US Neo-Nazi James Mason, who is listed as a terrorist entity by the Canadian government.

items.title: New Hollywood film excites as Philippine culture hits the big screen
items.summary: As animated film Forgotten Island gets ready to hit the big screen later this year, could it signal an end to Filipino typecasting in Hollywood?

items.title: What to watch in May, from free speech fodder to Nic Cage as Spider-Noir
items.summary: Plus, Shaun Micallef dives into Australia's gambling problem and the Stranger Things creators back another sci-fi mystery.

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of patron saints, the Friends finale and a stone cold 90s club banger
items.summary: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

items.title: Auckland FC cruises past Adelaide United to reach maiden A-League Men's grand final
items.summary: Auckland becomes the first team from New Zealand to qualify for an A-League Men's season decider after making light work of Adelaide at Hindmarsh Stadium.

items.title: Swans hold on for gritty victory over Pies, Suns see off Port
items.summary: The ladder-leading Sydney Swans scrape past an undermanned but plucky Collingwood side, as Gold Coast defeats Port Adelaide in Darwin.

items.title: Developer David Trask bids $50,000 donation for a lunch with Queensland Deputy Premier Jarrod Bleijie
items.summary: The developer, David Trask, made the successful bid as a donation at lunch on Friday for the ruling LNP, held at the Brisbane Convention Centre and dubbed "Delivering for Queensland Industry Update".

items.title: Invasive 'barbecue stopper' Asian tiger mosquito detected away from entry ports on mainland for first time
items.summary: The world's most invasive mosquito has previously been found at borders, islands, and ports, but has just been discovered in a remote mainland Queensland community.
